title: gh-146250: Fix memory leak in re-initialization of `SyntaxError`
author: brijkapadia
body:
Added `Py_XDECREF`/`Py_XSETREF` to avoid a memory leak when calling `SyntaxError.__init__`.

<!--


Where: gh-NNNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```

```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, for example: [3.13].

GH-NNNNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: Sorry about the Py_CLEAR suggestion but I think creating new variables would be cleaner.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: I think it is just better to hold temp vars and use XSETREF like in `AttributeError.__init__`

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: I will test the refleak bots so don't commit anything until they are done!

comments.author: bedevere-bot
comments.body:
:robot: New build scheduled with the buildbot fleet by @picnixz for commit 22cff11dffc34d88d0303fcc447ae7e930760225 :robot:

Results will be shown at:

https://buildbot.python.org/all/#/grid?branch=refs%2Fpull%2F146251%2Fmerge

🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Sorry, @bkap123 and @kumaraditya303, I could not cleanly backport this to `3.13` due to a conflict.
Please backport using [cherry_picker](https://pypi.org/project/cherry-picker/) on command line.
```
cherry_picker 0de4e08a5990e4692feb1b1ea01c303e468a2894 3.13
```
